assertion_results.text: Test Quality verdict is WARN (not N/A or PASS) — the Eval Quality component is WARN because eval-3 has 2 failing assertions, and WARN in any component produces WARN in the combined Test Quality verdict
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: '| Test Quality | WARN | Eval Quality: WARN (eval-3: 2 failing assertions, 85% pass rate; overall: 54/56 assertions, 96% pass rate). Repetitive Test Detection: N/A. Test Documentation: N/A. |' — Test Quality is WARN, Eval Quality component is WARN due to eval-3's 2 failing assertions, and the WARN component produces the combined WARN verdict.

assertion_results.text: The eval result review from github-actions[bot] is correctly identified as an eval result — the report's Test Quality or Eval Quality section reflects eval metrics (pass rates, failing assertions) extracted from the eval review, confirming the orchestrator detected and processed it
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 29-36: 'Review 40001 from github-actions[bot] was identified as an eval result review using the 3-criteria heuristic' with all 3 criteria matching (author is github-actions[bot], body contains '## Eval Results' marker, body contains 'sdlc-workflow/run-evals' footer). Lines 39-48 show parsed metrics per eval (eval-1 through eval-5). Line 13 Test Quality row reflects eval metrics: '96% pass rate'. Line 60: 'Eval Quality Verdict: WARN'.

assertion_results.text: The human reviewer comment from reviewer-b (comment id 50001) is NOT misidentified as an eval result — it is processed as a normal review comment (classified in review-50001.md) and its classification does not reference eval detection or eval metrics
assertion_results.evidence: review-50001.md exists and classifies comment 50001 as 'Code Change Request' from reviewer-b. Line 9: 'Type: Inline comment (NOT an eval result)'. Lines 26-28 explicitly confirm: 'This is NOT an eval result review. It is a human reviewer comment from reviewer-b... It does not match any of the three eval result detection criteria'. The classification reasoning references directive language analysis, review state, and specificity — no eval metrics or eval detection references. report.md line 73: 'Comment 50001 | reviewer-b | Inline comment | Code change request'.

assertion_results.text: Eval pass rate and failing assertion details appear in the verification report's Test Quality row — the report includes eval-3's failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13 Test Quality row includes pass rate: 'eval-3: 2 failing assertions, 85% pass rate; overall: 54/56 assertions, 96% pass rate'. Lines 50-58 detail eval-3's two failing assertions: (1) 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion)' with evidence about missing CONVENTIONS.md lookup, and (2) 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path' with evidence about no sub-task being created.

assertion_results.text: An eval failure sub-task is created for failing eval-3 — a sub-task description file targets eval-3 assertion failures and includes the failing assertion text and evidence
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md exists with description: 'Fix eval-3 assertion failures related to convention upgrade eligibility evaluation and sub-task creation'. Review Context section (lines 34-41) quotes both failing assertions verbatim with their evidence text. report.md lines 87-90 confirm: 'Sub-task 1: Fix eval-3 assertion failures (eval failure sub-task)' with labels '["ai-generated-jira", "eval-failure"]' and trigger 'Eval Quality WARN with 2 regression-classified failing assertions in eval-3'.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include Review Context with the failing assertion text and evidence from the eval review — the Review Context section quotes the two failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation, including the evidence text explaining what was missing
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md lines 33-41: '## Review Context' section quotes both assertions: (1) 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion)...' with Evidence: 'The output file review-30002.md classifies the comment as a suggestion but does not evaluate convention upgrade eligibility -- no CONVENTIONS.md lookup or codebase pattern analysis is documented in the classification reasoning'; (2) 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path...' with Evidence: 'No sub-task was created for review comment 30002 -- it was classified as suggestion and no convention upgrade was attempted, so the suggestion was not elevated to a code change request'.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include a Target PR section pointing to the PR URL https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747 so that implement-task adds commits to the existing PR branch
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md line 43: '## Target PR' followed by 'https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'. subtask-2.md line 35: '## Target PR' followed by 'https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'. Both sub-task descriptions include the Target PR section with the correct URL.

assertion_results.text: Root-cause investigation runs on the created eval failure sub-tasks — the report includes a Root-Cause Investigation verdict that is not N/A, indicating the investigation pipeline processed the eval failure sub-tasks
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 6: '| Root-Cause Investigation | DONE | Eval failure sub-task investigated; convention upgrade eligibility gap traced to implement-task phase -- method-based skill gap (convention upgrade analysis must document its reasoning for every suggestion) |'. The verdict is 'DONE', not N/A. Lines 103-123 provide detailed investigation results including universality test, method-vs-fact test, classification as 'Skill gap (implement-task phase)', and investigation of the review feedback sub-task.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains exactly 11 files: report.md (verification report), review-50001.md (comment classification), subtask-1.md and subtask-2.md (sub-task descriptions), and criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md (acceptance criteria evaluations). All are verification/analysis output artifacts. No code files (e.g., .ts, .py, .rs, .go, .md skill files) were modified — the sub-task descriptions specify 'Files to Modify' as future instructions, not actual modifications.

assertion_results.text: Non-eval checks (Scope Containment, Sensitive Patterns, Acceptance Criteria) are unaffected by eval processing — Scope Containment compares the diff files against the task's Files to Modify, Sensitive Patterns scans the diff for secrets, and Acceptance Criteria evaluates each criterion against the diff content independently of the eval results
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7: Scope Containment 'PASS | PR modifies exactly the 2 files specified in the task' — compares diff against task spec, no eval reference. Line 10: Sensitive Patterns 'PASS | No secrets, credentials, API keys, or private keys detected in the diff' — scans diff for secrets, no eval reference. Line 12: Acceptance Criteria 'PASS | 7/7 criteria met' — evaluated per criterion. criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md each evaluate specific acceptance criteria against the diff content (symbol scanning, documentation comments, verdict rules, output format, verdict mapping) with zero references to eval results or eval metrics.
